phases.name: ReferenceRegistry walking skeleton
phases.description: Build the pure-domain ReferenceRegistry that the resolver and detection pipeline will consume in later waves.

steps.name: Stub buildRegistry empty contract
criteria: Importing the registry module no longer fails
buildRegistry on empty config produces empty byName
buildRegistry on empty config produces empty byFilePath
Returned registry version is strictly greater than zero
steps.scenario: Loading state with no aggregated configuration renders no tokens and no crash
steps.scenario_name: buildRegistry returns an empty registry when given an empty aggregated config
steps.implementation_notes: Create the module with the public surface from architecture sec 6.1: types RefType, RegistryEntry, ReferenceRegistry; pure functions buildRegistry, lookupByName, lookupByPath. Empty implementation: buildRegistry returns empty maps and version prevVersion+1.

steps.name: Index AggregatedConfig into byName
criteria: Building from walkingSkeletonConfig and looking up nw-bdd-requirements returns one entry
Returned entry has type skill scope user name nw-bdd-requirements
lookupByName for an unknown name still returns an empty list
buildRegistry remains synchronous and pure
steps.scenario: lookupByName returns the user-scope skill nw-bdd-requirements as a single live entry
steps.scenario_name: lookupByName returns the user-scope skill nw-bdd-requirements as a single live entry
steps.implementation_notes: Walk all seven AggregatedConfig collections (agents parsed branch only commands skills hooks mcpServers rules plugins) and produce RegistryEntry per item keyed in byName as a multimap. Mirror configAggregator.ts style.

steps.name: Populate byFilePath with normalisation
criteria: lookupByPath with the absolute href returns a non-null entry
Returned entry has type skill name nw-bdd-requirements
Path equivalence is structural across forms
lookupByPath returns null for an unknown path
steps.scenario: lookupByPath resolves the absolute markdown link href to the same registry entry
steps.scenario_name: lookupByPath resolves the absolute markdown link href to the same registry entry
steps.implementation_notes: Add a private normalisePath string transform handling backslash forward slash conversion leading dot slash strip interior dot slash collapse trailing slash strip. Apply at insert and lookup. Do not call node:os.

steps.name: Preserve multi-scope name collisions
criteria: Looking up release in ambiguousReleaseConfig returns at least two candidates
Returned candidate scopes include both project and user
Single-entry lookups still return exactly one entry
Candidate ordering is deterministic across builds
steps.scenario: lookupByName for an ambiguous name returns all matching candidates
steps.scenario_name: lookupByName for an ambiguous name returns all matching candidates
steps.implementation_notes: byName multimap from 01-02 should already preserve multiple entries. Behavioural validation step. Do not sort here ordering policy belongs to ScopePrecedence.

steps.name: NFR-2 500-item registry build
criteria: make500ItemConfig returns an AggregatedConfig with 500 items
buildRegistry on the 500-item config produces a registry with byName size 500
Build completes within 100 ms wall-clock
buildRegistry returns a value not a Promise
All previously-live scenarios continue to pass
steps.scenario: buildRegistry with 500 items completes synchronously and produces the correct entry count
steps.scenario_name: buildRegistry with 500 items completes synchronously and produces the correct entry count
steps.implementation_notes: Add make500ItemConfig helper to fixtures.ts producing 100 each of skills commands agents hooks mcpServers with unique names. Un-skip the scenario. Use performance.now for elapsed timing.

phases.name: ReferenceResolver driving port
phases.description: Pure classifier that turns a detected Reference into live ambiguous dead or unsupported using the registry.

steps.name: Stub resolver with live outcome for single-name match
criteria: Importing resolve from the resolver module no longer fails
resolve of a known name returns tag live with the matching entry
Returned entry has the expected type scope and name
Resolver is pure and synchronous
steps.scenario: Resolving a reference whose name matches a single registry entry returns the live outcome
steps.scenario_name: Resolving a reference whose name matches a single registry entry returns the live outcome
steps.implementation_notes: Create resolver.ts. Public types Reference with kind name or path and a value string plus ResolvedRef tagged union per architecture section 6.3. Implement resolve. For kind name dispatch to lookupByName. Single-result returns tag live with entry. Note contract deviation from architecture section 6.3 Reference shape will be back-propagated to DESIGN in finalize.

steps.name: Ambiguous outcome when name matches multiple entries
criteria: resolve of a name with multiple entries returns tag ambiguous
candidates list has length at least two
candidate scopes include both project and user
Ordering is deterministic across calls for the same registry
steps.scenario: Resolving a reference whose name matches two or more registry entries returns the ambiguous outcome
steps.scenario_name: Resolving a reference whose name matches two or more registry entries returns the ambiguous outcome
steps.implementation_notes: When lookupByName returns two or more entries return tag ambiguous with the candidates array unchanged. No sorting ordering policy belongs to ScopePrecedence.

steps.name: Dead outcome when name is unknown
criteria: resolve of an unknown name returns tag dead
searchedScopes is a non-empty list of ConfigScope values
Dead resolution does not throw
Existing live and ambiguous outcomes still pass
steps.scenario: Resolving a reference that matches no registry entry returns the dead outcome with the searched scopes
steps.scenario_name: Resolving a reference that matches no registry entry returns the dead outcome with the searched scopes
steps.implementation_notes: When lookupByName and lookupByPath both miss return tag dead with searchedScopes. Source of scopes is the ConfigScope literal union user project plugin.

steps.name: Unsupported outcome for path kinds the plugin does not expose
criteria: resolve of a path not under a supported item type returns tag unsupported
Returned outcome carries the original path
Returned outcome carries a non-empty reason string
Path-kind references to supported item types still resolve as live
steps.scenario: Resolving a file-path reference to an item type the plugin does not expose returns the unsupported outcome
steps.scenario_name: Resolving a file-path reference to an item type the plugin does not expose returns the unsupported outcome
steps.implementation_notes: For kind path call lookupByPath. If it misses classify as unsupported when the path suggests a known-but-unexposed item type otherwise dead. Simple heuristic for v1 paths under .claude that do not match a supported category return unsupported with a reason naming the missing category.

phases.name: NavHistory LRU-50 stack
phases.description: Pure LRU-50 navigation history. Powers Alt+Left and Alt+Right per ADR-006.

steps.name: Stub NavHistory with goBack happy path
criteria: Importing pushEntry goBack goForward canGoBack canGoForward from the history module no longer fails
goBack on a 4-entry history with headIndex 3 returns headIndex 2
Entries array is unchanged by goBack
Module is pure and synchronous
steps.scenario: Alt+Left restores the previous navigation snapshot
steps.scenario_name: goBack on a 4-entry history with headIndex=3 returns headIndex=2
steps.implementation_notes: Create history.ts with types NavEntry and NavHistory per ADR-006. Entries array and headIndex number. Exports pushEntry goBack goForward canGoBack canGoForward all pure. NavEntry can be any readonly record for now concrete shape in future waves. Add makeHistoryWith4Entries and emptyHistory helpers to fixtures.ts. goBack decrements headIndex when greater than 0 else returns the same history.

steps.name: goForward happy path
criteria: goForward on a 4-entry history with headIndex 2 returns headIndex 3
Entries array is unchanged by goForward
goBack from 03-01 continues to pass
steps.scenario_name: goForward on a 4-entry history with headIndex=2 returns headIndex=3
steps.implementation_notes: goForward increments headIndex when less than entries.length minus 1 else returns the same history. Symmetric to goBack.

steps.name: pushEntry truncates forward stack before pushing
criteria: pushEntry on a history with headIndex less than entries.length minus 1 truncates the tail
New entry appended after truncation
headIndex advances to new last index
Previously live scenarios continue to pass
steps.scenario: A new cross-reference action after Alt+Left clears the forward stack
steps.scenario_name: pushEntry on a history with headIndex < entries.length-1 truncates the tail before pushing
steps.implementation_notes: pushEntry takes history and an entry. Truncate entries to entries.slice(0, headIndex + 1) then concat the new entry. New headIndex is the new last index. LRU cap handled in 03-06 and 03-07.

steps.name: goBack at start of history is a no-op
criteria: goBack on a history with headIndex 0 returns the same history by structural equality
canGoBack returns false when headIndex is 0
goBack does not throw
Previously live scenarios continue to pass
steps.scenario: Alt+Left at the start of history is a no-op with end-of-history cue
steps.scenario_name: goBack on a history with headIndex=0 returns the same history
steps.implementation_notes: goBack already had the guard from 03-01 this step adds canGoBack as a pure predicate. canGoBack returns headIndex greater than 0. The end-of-history cue itself lives in the provider effect not in this module.

steps.name: goForward at end of history is a no-op
criteria: goForward on a history with headIndex equal to entries.length minus 1 returns the same history
canGoForward returns false at the end
goForward does not throw
Previously live scenarios continue to pass
steps.scenario: Alt+Right at end of history is a no-op
steps.scenario_name: goForward on a history with headIndex=entries.length-1 returns the same history
steps.implementation_notes: Symmetric to 03-04. canGoForward returns headIndex less than entries.length minus 1.

steps.name: LRU 50 invariant as a property test
criteria: For any sequence of pushEntry goBack goForward actions the entries length stays at most 50
headIndex is always within valid bounds
fast-check runs with default numRuns not loop-of-one
Existing example-based scenarios continue to pass
steps.scenario: For any sequence of navigation actions the history never exceeds 50 entries
steps.scenario_name: property: |entries| <= 50 and 0 <= headIndex < |entries| after any sequence of pushEntry/goBack/goForward
steps.implementation_notes: pushEntry enforces the LRU cap. When entries.length equals 50 drop entries[0] before appending. headIndex adjusts so it still points at the new last entry. Use fast-check fc.array of an action arbitrary union of pushEntry goBack goForward. Initial state is emptyHistory. Reduce actions then assert invariants.

steps.name: LRU eviction drops oldest and pins headIndex
criteria: pushEntry on a 50-entry history evicts entries 0
entries length remains 50
New entry is at index 49
headIndex remains 49 after eviction
Existing scenarios continue to pass
steps.scenario: LRU eviction at cap of 50 evicts the oldest entry and shifts headIndex
steps.scenario_name: pushEntry on a 50-entry history at headIndex=49 evicts entries[0] and stays at headIndex=49
steps.implementation_notes: The LRU behaviour is already implemented from 03-06. This step un-skips the specific eviction scenario to lock in the contract with an example. Add makeFiftyEntries helper to fixtures.ts.
